preview_text:
The model favors Germany at 35%, but the margin over Ecuador is narrow—only 2 percentage points separate the two. Ecuador's 33% win probability positions it as a genuine contender rather than an outsider, suggesting a competitive matchup at a neutral venue.

The draw probability of 32% is substantial, indicating the model expects a tightly contested contest where stalemate is a plausible outcome. The three outcomes cluster closely, with no probability exceeding 36%, reflecting genuine uncertainty about which side will prevail. This is a match the model assesses as genuinely competitive rather than one-sided.

The market and model diverge considerably. Sportsbooks assign Germany a 55% win probability—20 percentage points higher than the model—while pricing Ecuador at just 20%, roughly 13 points lower. Polymarket shows near-equal odds across all three outcomes at 33% each, closer to the model's view than to sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the substantial gaps warrant attention from users interpreting the forecast.
